initial_prompt_contains: Starter seed: The Black Lantern warns of a debt collected at dawn.
Remember: the harbor master owes the party a favor.

arguments.query: Fear die Duality Fear reference Daggerheart

arguments.content:
Remember: the harbor master owes the party a favor.

Session notes — Opening Night (2026-03-18):
- The Black Lantern warned the party that a debt will be collected at dawn.
- Harbor debt: an unnamed creditor (or collectors) is expected to come to the harbor to collect; the debt is due and may involve coercion or violence.
- The harbor master is indebted to the party — this can be used as leverage, a friendly contact, or a plot hook.
- GM intent: open the scene at the harbor before dawn, with the party positioned to intervene or negotiate. Track any countdowns or Fear spent if collectors escalate the situation.

arguments.description: Pre-dawn at the eastern quay of Replay Harbor. Salt-slick planks, stacked crates, and the low murmur of watchmen. Lantern light pools in rings; gulls roost on rigging. The Black Lantern's warning hangs in the air: a debt is to be collected at dawn. Tension and possible collectors arriving should be immediately present. The harbor master is a known ally of the party and may be found near his office by the docks.
arguments.name: Dawn at the Quay

arguments.text:
The quay breathes in slow, salt-sour gusts beneath a pale, thinning sky. Lanterns wobble along the planks; crates cast long, spidery shadows. Near the harbor master's office, a broad-shouldered man leans on the rail, eyes red-rimmed with worry — the same harbor master who owes you a favor. He gives you a quick, guilty nod when he sees you.

From the east, the Black Lantern's warning hangs like a smear in your head: a debt is due at dawn. Voices — low, hard — drift across the water. Footsteps on the gangway. Someone has come to collect.

You have a few breaths before the collectors make their presence known. You can step forward to speak with the harbor master, confront whoever arrives, set a trap, try to learn who the collectors answer to, or take some other preparation. Choose your approach and describe what your character does.

arguments.frame_text: Player phase — Readiness Character 1: You're on the quay before dawn. Describe your approach (speak to the harbor master, confront arrivals, hide, set a trap, gather information, etc.). If your action requires a roll, describe how you want to attempt it; I'll set the Difficulty and call for a roll when needed.

steps.output_text:
OOC — I consulted the Daggerheart "Running an Adventure" guidance (Fear usage and GM moves) and updated campaign memory with session notes about the harbor debt.

Session memory updated (memory.md):
- Existing: "Remember: the harbor master owes the party a favor."
- Added session notes: The Black Lantern warned the party that a debt will be collected at dawn; unnamed collectors are expected; harbor master is an ally/lever; GM intends to open at the quay before dawn and track countdowns/Fear as collectors escalate.

Active scene created and set:
- Scene: Dawn at the Quay (pre-dawn on the eastern quay; harbor master present; collectors expected)

Authoritative in-character narration (committed to the scene):
"The quay breathes in slow, salt-sour gusts beneath a pale, thinning sky. Lanterns wobble along the planks; crates cast long, spidery shadows. Near the harbor master's office, a broad-shouldered man leans on the rail, eyes red-rimmed with worry — the same harbor master who owes you a favor. He gives you a quick, guilty nod when he sees you.

From the east, the Black Lantern's warning hangs like a smear in your head: a debt is due at dawn. Voices — low, hard — drift across the water. Footsteps on the gangway. Someone has come to collect.

You have a few breaths before the collectors make their presence known. You can step forward to speak with the harbor master, confront whoever arrives, set a trap, try to learn who the collectors answer to, or take some other preparation. Choose your approach and describe what your character does."

Player phase started (acting character: Readiness Character 1)
GM frame to the player: Describe your approach (speak to the harbor master, confront arrivals, hide, set a trap, gather information, etc.). If your action requires a roll, describe how you want to attempt it and I will set the Difficulty and call for the roll when needed.

If you want any OOC notes, to pause the session, or to adjust fear/countdown bookkeeping before the player acts, tell me now.
